task.name: review-continuity

metadata.description: Review visual continuity across scene boundaries. Extracts boundary frames and checks for consistency in character appearance, lighting, and environment.

input.name: assets_directory
input.description: Episode assets directory containing scene video clips.

input.name: ssd
input.description: Scene Specification Document for scene ordering and context.

output.description: Continuity report with pass/warn/fail for each scene boundary pair.

step.name: Discover Scene Clips
step.description: Identify and order all scene video clips for boundary analysis.

actions.action: If SSD is available, use scene order from scene-spec.yaml to determine sequence
If SSD is not available, sort by scene_id (alphanumeric) or filename convention
Use only the latest version of each scene's clip (highest version number)
Verify at least 2 clips exist (need at least one boundary to check)

step.name: Extract Boundary Frames
step.description: For each consecutive scene pair, extract the last frame of scene N and first frame of scene N+1.

actions.action:
For each consecutive pair (scene_N, scene_N+1):
Extract LAST frame of scene_N: ffmpeg -y -sseof -0.04 -i "{scene_N_clip}" -frames:v 1 -q:v 2 "{output_dir}/{scene_N}_last.png"
This seeks to 0.04 seconds before the end of the clip to capture the final visible frame.

Extract FIRST frame of scene_N+1: ffmpeg -y -i "{scene_N+1_clip}" -frames:v 1 -q:v 2 "{output_dir}/{scene_N+1}_first.png"
This captures the very first frame of the next scene.
Store the frame pair: {pair_id, scene_n_id, scene_n1_id, last_frame_path, first_frame_path}

step.name: Character Continuity
step.description: Assess character appearance consistency across each boundary.

actions.action:
For each frame pair, analyze: - Character presence: same characters visible in both frames? - Character appearance: consistent clothing, hairstyle, accessories? - Character position: logically consistent with scene transition? - Character scale: consistent relative size?
Scoring: - "pass": characters are visually consistent - "warn": minor differences (e.g., slight color shift) - "fail": major inconsistency (missing character, wrong outfit)
Note: If SSD indicates a deliberate location change, adjust expectations.

step.name: Lighting Continuity
step.description: Assess lighting and color grading consistency across each boundary.

actions.action:
For each frame pair, analyze: - Overall brightness: similar exposure levels? - Color temperature: consistent warm/cool tone? - Color palette: similar dominant colors? - Contrast: similar dynamic range? - Time of day: if same period, lighting should match
Scoring: - "pass": lighting is consistent - "warn": noticeable but minor lighting shift - "fail": drastic lighting change without narrative justification

step.name: Environment Continuity
step.description: Assess environment and background consistency across each boundary.

actions.action:
For each frame pair, analyze: - Setting: same location? If so, visually consistent? - Background elements: consistent background objects? - Weather/atmosphere: consistent sky, fog, rain? - Perspective: logically continuous camera angle/distance?
Scoring: - "pass": environment consistent or intentional transition per SSD - "warn": minor environmental discrepancy - "fail": unexpected environment change within same declared location

step.name: Compile Report
step.description: Determine overall status for each boundary pair and compile the report.

actions.action: For each pair, overall_status = - "pass": all three checks (character, lighting, environment) pass - "warn": at least one check is "warn" but none are "fail" - "fail": at least one check is "fail"

Episode-level summary: - total_boundaries: count of scene pairs - passed: count of "pass" pairs - warned: count of "warn" pairs - failed: count of "fail" pairs - continuity_score: (passed / total_boundaries) * 100 - overall_status: "PASS" if failed == 0, "WARN" if only warnings, "FAIL" if any failures
Keep extracted boundary frames in episodes/{episode_id}/validation/boundary-frames/ for manual review

task.output_schema:
# continuity-report.yaml
episode_id: "{episode_id}" assets_path: "episodes/{episode_id}/assets/" reviewed_at: "{ISO-8601 timestamp}"
summary: total_boundaries: {int} passed: {int} warned: {int} failed: {int} continuity_score: {float, 0-100} overall_status: "{PASS|WARN|FAIL}"
scene_pairs: - pair_id: "{scene_N_id}->{scene_N+1_id}" scene_n: scene_id: "{scene_N_id}" clip_path: "{relative path}" last_frame: "{path to extracted frame}" scene_n_plus_1: scene_id: "{scene_N+1_id}" clip_path: "{relative path}" first_frame: "{path to extracted frame}" overall_status: "{pass|warn|fail}" checks: character_continuity: status: "{pass|warn|fail}" details: "{findings}" lighting_continuity: status: "{pass|warn|fail}" details: "{findings}" environment_continuity: status: "{pass|warn|fail}" details: "{findings}" notes: "{additional observations}"
recommendations: - pair: "{scene_N_id}->{scene_N+1_id}" issue: "{description}" suggestion: "{recommended fix}"
boundary_frames_directory: "episodes/{episode_id}/validation/boundary-frames/"

error.action: HALT: "Assets directory not found at episodes/{episode_id}/assets/"
HALT: "At least 2 scene clips required for continuity review. Found {count}."
HALT: "FFmpeg is required for frame extraction but not found in PATH."
WARN: "Failed to extract frames for boundary {scene_N}->{scene_N+1}. Skipping." Continue with remaining pairs.
WARN: "Scene spec not found. Using filename-based ordering. Intentional-transition detection unavailable."
WARN for affected pairs. Mark as "error" status. Continue with remaining pairs.
